summary: Demonstrates the Phase 2.6 (Improvement 3) uncovered-risk reporting. Two risks: RISK-001 (high) is covered and passing; RISK-002 (high) has NO test case at all. The explicit uncovered_risks fields surface the gap, and because a high-severity risk is uncovered the recommendation is conditional_pass (not pass), with the gap named as a condition.
release_recommendation_reasoning: All executed tests passed and RISK-001 is covered_passing, but RISK-002 (high severity) has no covering test case at all (uncovered_high_severity_count = 1). A high-severity risk with zero tests is a coverage gap, so this cannot be a clean pass. Condition: add at least one test case for RISK-002 (re-open Gate 2) before release, or record an explicit acceptance decision for it.
open_questions: RISK-002 is uncovered (high severity) — decide whether to add a test case or formally accept the risk without one.
